item.title: EA Game is Shutting Down on June 24
item.description:
This article was updated by Andrea Trama on April 8, 2026, clearing up the list of EA games shutting down in 2026. It was first published on April 4.

Plants vs. Zombies HD for Apple has been added to the list of games EA will be shutting down in 2026, with its last date for service planned for June 24. The quirky tower defense under the EA banner has been around since May 2009, but the end of the line is nearing quickly for players on Apple computers and devices.

Despite reaching massive success in the early days of the smartphone era, the Plants vs. Zombies games have had a harder time connecting with fans in recent years. As the latest brand-new entry in the mainline series, Plants vs. Zombies 3 was delisted from iOS and Google Play in November 2024 because it was in need of a major overhaul based on player feedback, receiving only a soft re-launch in 2025.

All EA Games Listed for Shutdown in 2026 (So Far)

Past

  • Anthem (Xbox One, PlayStation 4, EA App) - January 12
  • The Sims Mobile (iOS, Android) - January 20
  • NBA Live 19 (Xbox One, PlayStation 4) - January 30
  • Real Racing 3 (Apple, Google, Amazon, Facebook) - March 20

Upcoming

  • Plants vs. Zombies Garden Warfare (PlayStation 3) - April 28
  • EA Sports FC Tactical (Apple, Google, Facebook) - May 7
  • Plants vs. Zombies HD (Apple) - June 24
  • Madden NFL 23 (Xbox One, Xbox X/S, PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, EA App, Steam) - July 13
  • NHL 22 (PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, Xbox One, and Xbox Series X/S) - August 31
  • NHL 23 (PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, Xbox One, and Xbox Series X/S) - August 31

Plants vs Zombies HD has now been added to the list of games that EA will be ending service for in the near future, but it isn't the only game in the franchise in that group, and the other is coming up on its big farewell soon. On April 28, EA will be shutting down the PlayStation 3 version of Plants vs. Zombies Garden Warfare , which was first added to PlayStation in August 2014. Being that it was the first Plants vs. Zombies game featuring third-person shooter gameplay, the genre shift didn't draw in as many fans as the original tower defense formula, but the game still managed to resonate relatively well with players and critics alike, even earning itself a sequel in 2016.

Although the end of service for two different games within a few months of each other may seem like an ill omen for the Plants vs. Zombies fandom, players probably shouldn't expect the franchise to be in trouble. In October 2025, Plants vs. Zombies: Replanted was released on multiple platforms, giving players a remastered version of the original game with a bunch of extras tacked on for good measure. In addition to improving the visuals, these additional features include a permadeath R.I.P. Mode, a challenging, non-linear Cloudy Day Mode that makes plants go to sleep when rain clouds pass overhead, local co-op and versus multiplayer gameplay, and the ability to speed up gameplay at will.

This year will also see the end of at least seven different EA Sports games, with the potential for more on the horizon. Already, Madden NFL 24 and NBA Live 19 have been shut down on various platforms, with their final days falling on February 17 and January 30, respectively. Upcoming shutdowns of EA's realistic athletic games have also been announced, including EA Sports FC Tactical on May 7, Madden NFL 23 on July 13, and both the 2022 and 2023 entries in EA's NHL series, which will both be ending service on current and past-gen Xbox and PlayStation consoles on August 31. Additionally, fans of EA's motorsports games should be prepared to say goodbye to F1 23 on EA Play, as its last day is planned for April 15.

Find all 10 pairs

Results

0
—
More Games
Find all 10 pairs
Results
0
—
More Games

Fans of fantasy combat, sci-fi shooters, and retro mysteries may also want to check EA's shutdown calendar to make sure their games will still be waiting for them. Rounding out the list of EA games nearing the end of service in 2026 are story-heavy indie walking simulator Gone Home , which leaves the EA Play Pro list on April 27; BioWare action-RPG Dragon Age: Inquisition for PlayStation 3 on April 28, realistic FPS Battlefield Hardline for Xbox One and PlayStation 4 on June 22, and the Nintendo Switch version of hero shooter Apex Legends , which is planned for shutdown on August 4. Additionally, the demo version of action-RPG Kingdoms of Amalur will no longer be available on the EA Play App after April 16.

9 /10

Plants vs. Zombies

Follow
Followed
Tower Defense
Systems
Released
May 5, 2009

WHERE TO PLAY

item.title: I Replayed These Nintendo Wii Classics, And They Are Still Perfect
item.description:
The Nintendo Wii built its name on motion controls, but its best games offered far more than quick fun. Going back to these classics today shows how well they were put together. Many of them still feel smooth, focused, and easy to get into, even after so many years. These games do not rely on long tutorials or complicated systems. They trust the player to learn by playing, and that approach still works today.

The visuals of these Wii games still hold up today, not because of power, but because of art direction. Colors, shapes, and atmosphere still carry weight today, making these games easy to return to without feeling outdated.

GameRant Quiz

—

QUESTION 1/1

Results

0

—

More Games
GameRant Quiz
Easy (15s)Medium (10s)Hard (5s)
—
QUESTION 1/1
Results
0
—
More Games

Metroid Prime Trilogy

The Danger of Being in Space Alone is Still There

  • Combines all three Metroid Prime games with motion controls tailored for the Wii.
  • First-person shooter with a lot of exploration, puzzles, and platforming.

Revisiting Metroid Prime Trilogy today still feels surprisingly modern, largely because its design never depended on trends that age poorly. The game is largely about moving through detailed alien worlds, scanning objects, and slowly unlocking new areas. That approach gives it a steady pace that still feels refreshing.

One thing that stands out even now is how natural the Wii controls feel. The trilogy applies motion aiming to the earlier games, allowing players to point and shoot with more freedom. That extra control makes combat more engaging without changing the original design too much. Each game in the trilogy also offers something slightly different. The first tries to balance exploration, the second increases difficulty, and the third introduces a faster pace and more direct storytelling. That variety keeps the full bundle from feeling repetitive.

Super Mario Galaxy 2

Still One of the Most Well-Designed Platformers

  • A platformer where Mario jumps across small planets using gravity-based mechanics.
  • Creative level design, precise movement, and constant new gameplay ideas.

Super Mario Galaxy 2 is one of the best Wii Mario games and still feels great today because it refuses to repeat itself. Almost every level introduces a new idea, uses it in a clever way, and then moves on before it gets old. That design choice keeps the experience exciting from start to finish. The gravity system is one of the biggest reasons it still feels unique. Mario runs around small planets, flips upside down, and jumps between floating worlds.

The controls are still tight, so even with unusual movement, everything feels predictable and fair. This balance between creativity and control is one of the game’s strongest qualities. The visuals of this Wii-exclusive game still hold up today because of strong art direction. The bright colors, imaginative planets, and smooth animations make the game look lively even without modern graphics power.

Xenoblade Chronicles

A Huge RPG World That Still Feels Far Ahead of What the Wii Should Handle

  • Its massive open areas and seamless exploration still feel great by today’s standards.
  • Its real-time combat and story pacing still hold up without needing updates or fixes.

Replaying Xenoblade Chronicles today makes it clear that the game did not age the way most Wii RPG titles did. It still feels smooth, ambitious, and surprisingly modern in how it handles scale, movement, and player freedom. Players explore huge areas built on the bodies of two massive titans. Even today, the scale of the environment still impresses. Seeing distant landmarks and then reaching them later creates a strong sense of progression.

The combat system mixes automatic attacks with player-controlled abilities called Arts. This creates a system where timing and positioning matter more than fast button pressing. Returning to Xenoblade Chronicles today shows how well its ideas were executed. Its world, systems, and story still connect in a way that feels complete and satisfying.

The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess

A Darker, More Grounded Zelda Journey That Still Feels Rich And Complete

  • Dungeon design and puzzle structure still feel satisfying and well-paced.
  • Dual gameplay styles keep the experience varied without losing focus.

The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess still feels strong today because of its darker tone and structured design. The game leans into a more serious atmosphere compared to other Zelda titles like The Legend of Zelda: Skyward Sword . The gameplay is basically about switching between Link’s human form and wolf form. As a wolf, players track scents and interact with the world differently. As a human, the focus shifts to combat and puzzle-solving. This mix keeps the experience from feeling repetitive.

Dungeons remain one of the game’s best features. Each dungeon introduces a new item and builds puzzles around it. Players learn how to use each tool step by step, which makes solving puzzles satisfying instead of frustrating. Visually, the game still works because of its focus on atmosphere. Lighting, environments, and character design create a consistent mood that still looks quite good today.

Resident Evil 4: Wii Edition (2007)

Wii Edition Still Feels Sharp and Responsive

  • Wii pointer aiming still feels fast, accurate, and easy to use.
  • Strong pacing and enemy design keep tension high even today.

Resident Evil 4: Wii Edition still feels smooth and exciting even after all these years. The game mixes action with survival elements, forcing players to think carefully about ammo, positioning, and enemy behavior. One of the biggest improvements in the Wii version is the control system. Motion aiming allows players to point directly at enemies, making shooting faster and more precise. This change improves the overall flow of combat without removing the challenge.

The Wii version looks very close to the original GameCube release and is quite sharper than the PlayStation 2 version, which had lower image quality. It also gives players more control options. Alongside motion aiming, the Classic Controller can be used, bringing back the traditional control style and the original laser sight for aiming. On top of that, the game feels a bit easier overall, and Leon’s movements are slightly quicker, which makes the action flow more smoothly.

Silent Hill: Shattered Memories

A Psychological Horror Experience That Still Feels Personal And Unpredictable

  • A psychological horror that removes combat and focuses on exploration and player choices.
  • Changes story elements based on player behavior, making each playthrough feel personal.

Going back to Silent Hill: Shattered Memories today makes it clear how different it still feels. Instead of relying on combat, the game removes weapons completely. The player explores, solves small puzzles, and runs when danger appears. That design choice still feels bold. The game focuses on atmosphere and psychological tension rather than fighting enemies.

What I particularly love about Silent Hill: Shattered Memories is how it tracks player behavior. Choices, movement patterns, and even small actions affect how the story unfolds. Characters, environments, and dialogue can change depending on how the player behaves. The chase sequences still feel tense. When enemies appear, there is no fighting back. Players must escape using the environment, which creates a different kind of fear. Silent Hill: Shattered Memories is one of the best horrors on the Wii.

MadWorld

Brutal, Comic-Book Styled Action Game That Still Looks And Plays Unlike Anything Else

  • Black-and-white comic style still looks striking and timeless.
  • Score-based combat encourages creative and varied attacks.

Replaying MadWorld in 2026 reminded me of how eye-catching the monochromatic visual style was. The game uses a black-and-white comic book look, with bright red blood as the only strong color. That design still looks striking because it avoids trying to look realistic. Players use chainsaws, environmental hazards, and improvised weapons to defeat enemies. The game rewards variety, encouraging players to defeat enemies in different ways to earn higher scores.

The over-the-top tone is another reason why I love the game. Commentary during fights adds humor and energy, making the action feel like a violent game show. This tone keeps the experience entertaining for such a violent game. Even today, MadWorld still feels bold. The art style of this Wii game has not aged; its combat is still fun, and its tone remains unique. It does not feel like anything else on the Wii, and that originality is why it still feels perfect today.

Sin & Punishment: Star Successor

High-Speed Rail Shooter That Still Demands Pure Skill And Focus

  • Fast-paced rail shooter.
  • Combines precise shooting with constant movement, creating intense and skill-based gameplay.

While many Wii titles show their age through clunky controls or slow pacing, Sin & Punishment: Star Successor still moves with sharp precision from start to finish. Everything about it is built around speed, reaction time, and control, and that focus is exactly why it still feels fun. As a rail shooter, the character moves forward automatically, while the player handles aiming, shooting, and dodging at the same time. That combination creates constant pressure. There is no downtime. Enemies fill the screen, attacks come from multiple directions, and survival depends on reading patterns quickly.

Coming back to it now, Star Successor feels just as sharp as it did at release. It does not rely on story or extra systems to stay interesting. It’s fun because the gameplay is fast, clean, and demanding in the best way. That kind of design does not age, and that is exactly why it still feels perfect today.

item.title: Xbox Making Big Changes to Achievements
item.description:
Xbox is making some big changes to its achievement system, and Insiders are getting a glimpse ahead of everyone else. Achievements have been a major part of the Xbox ecosystem for decades now, going all the way back to 2005. Achievements have been a point of pride among gamers, offering an incentive to put in some extra work while playing various games.

The Xbox brand is in the middle of a major transition at the moment. Xbox Series X/S has struggled to find the same level of success as past Microsoft consoles, and the company's higher-ups have looked for ways to make users excited again. This year has seen major shake-ups at the top, with new Xbox boss Asha Sharma taking the place of Phil Spencer. It remains to be seen what the future holds for the company, but fans are hoping that things can start to turn around.

Xbox Achievement Changes Revealed

Xbox has been making changes to its achievements throughout 2026, and the company has even more in store. Xbox Insiders are getting these changes right now, but they'll be rolled out to all players sometime in the future. For starters, when a player obtains 100% of achievements for a game, it will now be highlighted on their profile, making it easier to spot. Getting 100% of achievements in a game is a big point of pride, especially for tougher games like Elden Ring . It makes sense that some players would want to make sure their biggest accomplishments don't get lost among all the other games they've played.

In one of the more interesting changes, Xbox users will gain the ability to hide games that appear on their profile, starting later this month. Following the change, players who have put a lot of work into games they might be less proud of can make sure other people can't see it. It will also prevent other users from finding out just how many achievements the player might have missed or struggled to obtain in relatively easy games. The change might seem silly, but Xbox says that it's been one of the most often requested changes by users. In an era where gamers don't have nearly as much privacy as they used to, it's easy to see how some might embrace this change. Hidden games will not have a negative impact on the user's Gamerscore.

In March, Xbox started to offer more color customization options. With this new update, achievement notifications will now match the user's custom-designed color, making them more personalized. There will also be new animations that appear when the player obtains a classic or rare achievement.

Balance the critic averages

Critical Mass

Pick games to balance the averages.

Time: 00:00
Diff: —

Scales

Multiplier ×1.00
Left
Avg —
Right
Avg —

Results

0
—
More Games

Choose a game

Balance the critic averages
Easy (6)Medium (8)Hard (10)

Critical Mass

Pick games to balance the averages.
Time: 00:00
Diff: —

Scales

Multiplier ×1.00
Left
Avg —
Right
Avg —
Results
0
—
More Games
Choose a game

So far, the achievement changes have seen a lot of praise on social media, with many users noting that these options should have happened a long time ago. It's unlikely that this update will suddenly make Xbox a more viable brand to newcomers, but it should create a more pleasant experience. With plans underway for Xbox Series X/S successor Project Helix, the company will need all the goodwill it can get. New consoles are always a tough sell, especially with how RAM costs keep going up. Xbox has a long way to go when it comes to restoring faith in the brand, but every little win helps.

item.title: How to Get the Golden-Knotted Ancestral Bow in Crimson Desert
item.description:
The Golden Knotted Ancestral Bow is one of the most secretive weapons that players can find in Crimson Desert . While it's not the best bow to obtain, it's still one of the most fun, specifically because it puts you through a unique trap-filled encounter like you're Indiana Jones.

You can find the Golden Knotted Ancestral Bow in the top northern part of Pailune, inside the secret Pailune Ancestors' Ruins . Below, we'll share the exact location and the traps you need to watch out for so you don't die in your quest to obtain this bow.

Where to Get the Golden Knotted Ancestral Bow in Crimson Desert

  • The Golden Knotted Ancestral Bow can be found inside the Pailune Ancestors' Ruins, which is located north of the first "i" in "Silver Wolf Mountain."
  • We've tagged the exact location in the map image above so you won't get lost.
  • In the wooded areas of Silver Wolf Mountain, you'll find a hatch on the floor when facing north (which is also showcased above).
  • Once you enter, drop down the ladder to enter the Pailune Ancestors' Ruins, but be careful, this area is filled with traps, and we'll guide you through them below.
  • As soon as you drop down the ladder, head forward, and be quick, as the floor will collapse and you might fall into lava.
  • Approach the door to open it, and you will then find a room with spinning blades that will move left to right. Wait for them to stop, then dart to the end of the room.
  • There are now three platforms you need to jump to, which is made easier with Precision Jump, which is activated by holding L1/LB, aiming where you want to jump, and then pressing Square/X. Watch out for the braziers, which will sway from left to right.
  • When you reach the dead end, jump and triple Force Palm (R3) to climb the wall, where you will be met with a closed door.
  • Enter the door simply by approaching it, and you can find the Golden Knotted Ancestral Bow on the floor as your prize.

Golden Knotted Ancestral Bow Stats & Upgrades in Crimson Desert

Attack

19

Critical Rate

Lv 1

Refinement

3

Abyss Gears (5)

Attack 2

Critical Rate 1

Health Siphon Lv1

Empty Slot

Empty Slot

  • The fully upgraded Golden Knotted Ancestral Bow will have an Attack of 35 and a Critical Rate of Lv 1.
  • Out of all the bows we've found, these stats are a tad underwhelming, especially with the Critical Rate addition.
  • What aids the Golden Knotted Ancestral Bow is the 5 Abyss Gears that you can use, meaning there's plenty of potential.
  • Explosive arrows and infinite arrows can make any bow a great choice, and the Golden Knotted Ancestral Bow is quite the experience to collect, so it's up to you if you want it.

Remember, for the most part, the bow does not matter, but the arrows and Abyss Gears do. Explosive arrows and the best bow build can be found with this guide.

Crimson Desert

Followed

item.title: Genshin Impact Leaks Nicole's Full Ability Kit
item.description:
Thanks to early datamining, Genshin Impact players now have a full overview of Nicole's ability kit, as the 5-Star Pyro Catalyst is set to elevate Varka (and other hypercarries) to new heights. With Version 6.5 going live on April 8, 2026, Genshin Impact is entering its beta testing phase for Version 6.6 – a patch that promises to be entirely focused on the Hexerei. Not only is Nicole making her debut as a universal Hexerei Support, but Version 6.6 is also introducing Lohen and Prune: two complementary Hexerei characters.

Rumors initially painted Nicole as three things: a Pyro Shielder, a Hexerei Healer, and Varka's ideal teammate. It's no secret that Varka in Genshin Impact launched with underwhelming numbers and lacked synergy with a fair few of his teammates. He lacked a Lauma to his Nefer or an Escoffier to his Skirk. Version 6.6 will give Varka exactly that, as Nicole's ability kit lives up to the expectations set by her rumors, and arguably surpasses them in some respects.

Genshin Impact: Nicole Ability Kit Overview

  • 5-Star Pyro Catalyst
  • Made with Hexerei teams in mind, but flexible enough to work with other hypercarries (such as Lyney).
  • Elemental Skill: Unmanifest Light
    • Creates a Pyro Shield that scales with ATK, providing the active character with ATK, and boosting it further if they are on the field for more than three seconds, or if they are a Hexerei member.

  • Elemental Burst: Pilgrimage of the Heavenly Path
    • Deals Pyro damage and summons Mystical Projections, who deal coordinated attacks that scale with the active character's ATK. These attacks are of the same elemental type as the active character's, rather than Nicole's Pyro.

  • Utility Passive: Quiet Contemplation
    • While not in combat, Nicole's Charged Attack summons a Seelie that guides players to the nearest treasure chest, but only for zones in which the player has crafted its respective Treasure Compass.

According to Seele Leaks, the datamine reveals Nicole as a 5-Star Pyro Catalyst with an Elemental Skill that provides a shield to the active character while buffing their ATK. Notably, Nicole's Elemental Burst summons Mystical Projections that function as coordinated attacks similar to Shenhe's quills. Early theorycrafting compares Nicole to a 5-Star Bennett who shields rather than heals. However, there are a few restrictions for Nicole that define her niche. The biggest one relates to her being a Hexerei character in Genshin Impact .

When paired with a Hexerei teammate, Nicole's Mystical Projections deal additional Elemental Damage equal to 150% of the triggering character's ATK. Moreover, the ATK buff on Nicole's shield triggers immediately for Hexerei characters, while other hypercarries have to wait three seconds after entering the field. In theory, this makes Nicole incompatible with Mavuika teams in Genshin Impact , but Varka and Venti will favor her over Bennett as the sustain option. This restriction is lifted entirely with Nicole's C6.

A fun passive for Nicole turns her Charged Attack into a Treasure Compass in Genshin Impact , where she summons a Seelie that guides the player to the nearest treasure chest. This passive only works for regions that the players have made a Treasure Compass for, excluding Dragonspine once more. First impressions of Nicole have been overwhelmingly positive, and players are excited to see how her ability kit will perform during the beta test for Version 6.5. One thing that gives the community pause is Nicole's particle generation: both her Signature Weapon and the new Hexerei Artifact Set imply that Nicole will have a costly Elemental Burst.

Balance the critic averages

Critical Mass

Pick games to balance the averages.

Time: 00:00
Diff: —

Right

Results
0
—
More Games
Choose a game

Nicole is expected to make her debut during the first half of Version 6.6 and will likely be accompanied by either Lohen, Mavuika, or Durin. Players can prefarm for Nicole's ascension materials, but her talent materials will be locked behind the new Trounce Domain boss coming in Version 6.6. The upcoming patch for Genshin Impact , as well as Nicole's debut, is set to arrive on May 20, 2026.

8 /10

Genshin Impact

Follow
Followed
RPG
Action
Adventure
Gacha
Open-World
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 81 /100 Critics Rec: 87%
Released
September 28, 2020
ESRB
Teen / Alcohol Reference, Fantasy Violence, In-Game Purchases (Includes Random Items), Users Interact
Developer(s)
HoYoverse (Formerly miHoYo)
Publisher(s)
HoYoverse (Formerly miHoYo)
6 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL

Step into Teyvat, a vast world teeming with life and flowing with elemental energy.

You and your sibling arrived here from another world. Separated by an unknown god, stripped of your powers, and cast into a deep slumber, you now awake to a world very different from when you first arrived.

Thus begins your journey across Teyvat to seek answers from The Seven — the gods of each element. Along the way, prepare to explore every inch of this wondrous world, join forces with a diverse range of characters, and unravel the countless mysteries that Teyvat holds...

MASSIVE OPEN WORLD
Climb any mountain, swim across any river, and glide over the world below, taking in the jaw-dropping scenery each step of the way. And if you stop to investigate a wandering Seelie or strange mechanism, who knows what you might discover?

ELEMENTAL COMBAT SYSTEM
Harness the seven elements to unleash elemental reactions. Anemo, Electro, Hydro, Pyro, Cryo, Dendro, and Geo interact in all sorts of ways, and Vision wielders have the power to turn this to their advantage.

Will you vaporize Hydro with Pyro, electro-charge it with Electro, or freeze it with Cryo? Your mastery of the elements will give you the upper hand in battle and exploration.

BEAUTIFUL VISUALS
Feast your eyes on the world around you, with a stunning art style, real-time rendering, and finely tuned character animations delivering you a truly immersive visual experience. Lighting and weather all change naturally over time, bringing every detail of this world to life.

SOOTHING SOUNDTRACK
Let the beautiful sounds of Teyvat draw you in as you explore the expansive world around you. Performed by the London Philharmonic Orchestra, the soundtrack changes seamlessly with the time and gameplay to match the mood.

BUILD YOUR DREAM TEAM
Team up with a diverse cast of characters in Teyvat, each with their own unique personalities, stories, and abilities. Discover your favorite party combinations and level up your characters to help you conquer even the most daunting of enemies and domains.

JOURNEY WITH FRIENDS
Team up with friends across various platforms to trigger more elemental action, tackle tricky boss fights, and conquer challenging domains together to reap rich rewards.

As you stand atop the peaks of Jueyun Karst and take in the rolling clouds and vast terrain stretching out before you, you may wish to stay in Teyvat a little longer... But until you are reunited with your lost sibling, how can you rest? Go forth, Traveler, and begin your adventure!

Genre(s)
RPG, Action, Adventure, Gacha, Open-World
Powered by
